scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_default_accept_redirects (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_default_accept_redirects). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
rpm --quiet -q kernel-core
sed -i '/net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects/d' /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf
while IFS= read -r line; do : done

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to answer the question: "What is the command and arguments to run the remediation?" Based on the context: It's about remediation of disabling acceptance of ICMP redirects.

We have attempted three times. The first two attempts tried using `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects=0` but could not verify. Now we need a different approach. The recommendation says: "Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r"
